title: Understanding the CanvasKit-WASM Renderer for Figma-like UIs
purpose: To explain how the provided code implements a Figma-like UI renderer using CanvasKit-WASM, focusing on rendering shapes, text, gradients, and effects.
module_context: The code is a comprehensive renderer for UI elements, leveraging CanvasKit-WASM to draw shapes, text, and effects on a canvas. It handles various node types (rectangles, ellipses, text, etc.), fills (solid, gradients, images), strokes, and effects (shadows, blurs).

steps.content: Determine if the node has rounded corners (independent or uniform).
Iterate through the fills of the node.
Apply each fill using `applyFill` and draw the node's fill using `drawNodeFill`.
Render effects behind the node (e.g., drop shadows).
Iterate through the strokes of the node.
Apply stroke properties (color, width, cap, join, dash pattern) using `resolveStrokeColor`.
Draw the node's stroke using `drawNodeStroke`.
Render effects in front of the node (e.g., inner shadows, blur).
Check if the fill is bound to a variable and resolve it.
If the fill is solid, set the color using `resolveFillColor`.
If the fill is a gradient, call `applyGradientFill`.
If the fill is an image, call `applyImageFill`.
Extract gradient stops and transform.
Convert stops to CanvasKit color format.
Based on gradient type (linear, radial, angular, diamond), create the appropriate CanvasKit shader.
Set the shader on the fill paint.
Retrieve the image hash from the fill.
Check if the image is already cached; if not, load it from the graph.
Determine the scaling mode (fill, fit, etc.) and calculate source and destination rectangles.
Create a shader using the image and the calculated matrix.
Set the shader on the fill paint.
Check if fonts are loaded and a font provider is available.
If fonts are loaded, build a paragraph using `buildParagraph` and draw it.
If fonts are not loaded, draw the text directly using the default font.
Create a ParagraphStyle with text alignment, font family, size, weight, slant, letter spacing, decoration, and line height.
Create a ParagraphBuilder using the ParagraphStyle and font provider.
Process style runs to apply different styles to parts of the text.
Build the paragraph and layout it within the node's width.
